key_points: Some dorms lack AC and WiFi — considered 'character building' by veterans
Snyder Hall incident: university asked students to do unpaid moving labor
Freshmen complain loudly; alumni say they'd kill to have it back
key_quotes: RIP to the Six Pack with no AC, no WiFi, Fat Don's, and vibes.
The no AC makes you go out of your room and make friends and explore. It's only bad for about 3 weeks tops.
I would literally cut my arm off to be a freshman moving back into that tiny little dorm room.

key_points: Bar scene is active; Greek life exists but not required to socialize
Campus wildlife events (escaped cow, otters, geese) become unifying social moments
Final Four run sparked massive, genuine campus-wide celebration energy
key_quotes: If you don't see a motorized couch during your time at college you went to the wrong school.
Alma crowd is massive.
I've been a shut in for most of the time I've been here, too caught up in my own head.

key_points: Local food scene is hit-or-miss; multiple restaurants called dry, oily, or under-seasoned
Papa Del's pizza is a beloved local institution with deep alumni loyalty
Black Dog BBQ called overpriced and underwhelming by repeat visitors
key_quotes: This is lowkey the majority of restaurants in this town. The food is either dry, oily, or under-seasoned.
P Dels pizza is maybe a top 5 pizza place I have ever had in my life.
$37 (with a pint of Bell's Amber) before 20% tip. If I was the owner I'd concentrate [on quality].

key_points: Students openly discuss depression, isolation, and therapy on the subreddit
Suicide in PAR hall reported with minimal surrounding discussion
Community rallies around struggling students; bread-baking PhD thread became mental health anchor
key_quotes: my mental health has been rescued [by baby geese on campus].
Tonight, at the behest of my therapist, I went to a bar for the first time.
When I first made a sexual misconduct complaint to the university, I was just as unsure that they would hear me.

key_points: Illini Commitment program exists for low-income in-state students — seen as a real lifeline
Out-of-state cost is steep; in-state value is frequently cited as a major draw
University listed as avg $16,954/yr for income $48k-$75k bracket
key_quotes: I qualify for Illini Commitment.
University of Illinois ($50 app fee), in-state avg price $16,954.
I'm 40k in debt already from my last hiccup... I really, really need my economics degree.

key_points: ECE 391 called 'worst-run, shittily taught, hostile to learning' class by multiple students
CS acceptance rate is ~7%; CS+X programs ~19% — genuinely competitive, not a safety
Transferring into CS from other UIUC colleges described as 'basically impossible'
key_quotes: This man doesn't merely move quickly, he teleports. He reads through his slides as if everyone in the room already knows the material.
Broken autograder and testcases, absolutely no documentation or just straight up incorrect information, no respect for students' time.
Called them up and they said it'd be basically impossible to transfer into any CS major because of how popular it is.

key_points: University slow to act on Illini Republicans despite repeated racist and violent posts
LGBT content quietly scrubbed from sexual misconduct training with no announcement
Canvas was hacked; administration decisions around exams during outage caused widespread frustration
key_quotes: The university's hands are tied. It's going to be extremely difficult for the university to get involved due to the fear of the Trump admin.
This kind of quiet erasure is dangerous. When queer experiences are scrubbed out of trainings, it tells LGBT students their realities don't matter.
Something happened outside your control, and a person high up the ladder made a decision that you get to deal with.

key_points: Campus is genuinely beautiful — fog, aurora, and storm photos go viral regularly
MTD bus system is widely praised as exceptional for a college town
Tornado warnings are a real recurring event, not hypothetical midwest weather drama
key_quotes: It feels like home! (I'm originally from SF).
We truly have the best bus system.
No scaffolding? No construction? 2/10 accuracy, try better next time. [on a Minecraft campus build]

key_points: Top 5 public feeder school to Silicon Valley firms including Google, Meta, Microsoft
CS grads highly recruited; ranked above Berkeley on CSRankings.org at times
Nobel Prize alumni count places UIUC 15th in the world — often overlooked
key_quotes: University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign [listed among] Top 5 Publics and Public feeders to Silicon Valley.
University of Illinois (30 Nobel laureates) — 15th in the world.
UIUC now outranks UC Berkeley for Computer Science [on CSRankings.org].

key_points: In-state tuition is one of the strongest value propositions in Big Ten
Out-of-state students paying premium prices for a school with a 'college town' location
University charges students tuition fees that fund RSOs doing hateful activity
key_quotes: Consolation prize: an amazing education from a top 25 school.
Reminder: Illini Republicans receives THOUSANDS of dollars of university funding as an RSO [paid by student fees].
10 years later new international students will stumble upon this and think '1b0b for $2000 isn't too bad' — the thought alone makes me shudder.

key_points: Genuinely tight-knit community — alumni stay emotionally connected for decades
Subreddit culture is wholesome chaos: bread baking, fishing reports, escaped livestock
Deep pride in school despite real frustrations with admin and politics
key_quotes: I love being an Illini and consider it like a huge family in a way.
I enjoy following this sub as someone who does not have any direct association with UIUC because of things like this.
Thank you UIUC — You have changed my life.

key_points: **Campus Safety:** Drive-by shooting near Cane's restaurant scared students inside the building
**Political Climate:** Illini Republicans posted AI-generated execution images and anti-immigrant content, funded by student fees
**Safety History:** Yingying Zhang murder (2017) still haunts community; sexual assault in student apartments documented by alumni
key_quotes: Report of a drive by shooting with one person shot in the abdomen. Two suspects reportedly fled southbound towards Joe's Brewery.
An AI image of a real person being executed — that's actually fucked.
A dear friend of mine was brutally attacked and raped in her Champaign student apartment in the 90s. She barely survived.

key_points: Fishing around Champaign County is shockingly excellent — musky, walleye, crappie all catchable
Parcadia (fantasy mini-golf bar with escape room puzzles) feels impossible it exists in a college town
MTD drivers are unusually kind and community-minded — a genuinely special campus transit culture
key_quotes: Parcadia and the escape rooms next door are genuinely so good it feels almost impossible they exist in CU.
Caught approximately 230 fish in the last 15 days, including... muskellunge, walleye, black crappie.
Happy holidays!! Thanks for your hard work [to the MTD bus driver who posted holiday wishes to riders].
